title: Check if config files exist before buildah command

comments.body: Ugh, looks like the test system is failing on every test due to your change.  It's not able to find some of the files in the test system such as policy.json.  We pass that in from the command line.  Not sure off the top of my head how to get around that.
@TomSweeneyRedHat is it reasonable if I just log the error message rather than directly exit(1) in main.go?
@QiWang19 I'd like @rhatdan to weigh in on just throwing the error but not exiting there.  But for a quick test, I think it would be OK to do just to see if it gets past the Production Travis CI.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 3e175b1) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
I am leading toward @mtrmac I don't think this is necessary.

I noted in #1307 that I think at least a warning message for registries.conf and policy.json non-existence would be good here.   I think the message that you get now are either not terribly clear (can't find the registry in the file when the file doesn't exist), or verbose and have to be waded through.  I think a warning for any command would help a user when things are out of whack and in reality rarely be issued.  

My $.02, I'm good with going with the general consensus.

Oh, the situation can _definitely_ be improved[1] - it’s just that this PR has the combination of _both_ being imprecise about the problem (only checking file presence vs. expected properties) _and_ being imprecise about the context (checking even for commands that don’t care at all). Fixing this to be precise about at least one of the two would make it much more acceptable to me.

I would be perfectly fine with adding this information to the output of `buildah info`, or maybe even `buildah version`; but making `buildah version`, and even `buildah --help`, report errors before doing anything else, is a bit much I think.

---

[1] Arguably the existence of `errors.Wrapf` leads to implementation-focused instead of end-user-focused errors, and is the underlying problem… but we’ve been very much benefiting from how much easier it makes to report …_not useless_ errors, so ripping that out in favor of thoughtful errors throughout is not likely to happen.

improve a little bit, check file presence for storage.conf and registries.conf, check expected properties for policy.json by calling signature.DefaultPolicy() (get rid of the vendor PR [#575](https://github.com/containers/image/pull/575)
But directly exit in main.go.
